zh.lifeSummary: 當William Robert Annesley II於1475年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾盧丁頓時，他的父親Thomas Annesley45歲，母親Agnes Clifton45歲。他於1502年與Mabellia English在英國巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1541年在英國諾廷加姆爾享年66歲。

nameMeanings.name: Annesley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Ansley
meanings: habitational name from such places as Ansley in Warwickshire or Annesley in Nottinghamshire. The former is named with Old English ānsetl ‘hermitage’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’; the latter with an Old English personal name An (‘the solitary one’) + lēah. In North America, the surname may in some cases also be an altered form of Scottish Ainslie . Compare Ainsley .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When William Robert Annesley II was born in 1475, in Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Thomas Annesley, was 45 and his mother, Agnes Clifton, was 45. He married Mabellia English in 1502, in Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 6 daughters. He died in 1541, in Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 66.

ko.lifeSummary: William Robert Annesley II(이)가 1475년 영국 노틴그함실르 루딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Annesley(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Clifton(은)는 45세였다. 그는 1502년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 Mabellia English(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1541년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.
